title: Initial Template Scaffold
message: First release of the Catherine full-stack template — a production-grade Express v5 + React 19 foundation with clean layered architecture and a standardized API contract.

whatChanged.text: Established the full-stack project structure
items: React 19 + Tailwind CSS v4 frontend with the Aumovio Design System
Node.js + Express v5 backend with a class-based OOP architecture

whatChanged.text: Defined the frontend three-layer architecture (api → hook → view)
Defined the backend layered architecture (Route → Controller → Service → Model)
Added a standardized API response shape (sendSuccess / sendError)
Added the AppError class and a single global error handler

title: Security Middleware Suite
message: Hardened the template with a full suite of security middleware covering response headers, CSRF, CORS, IP filtering, and scanner blocking.

whatChanged.text: Added Helmet for secure HTTP response headers
Added double-submit-cookie CSRF protection
Added network-aware CORS with corporate, VPN, and local origin matching
Added a CIDR-aware IP allowlist filter
Added a security filter that blocks scanners, path traversal, and script injection
Added redirect prevention for all /api routes

title: JWT Authentication & Dynamic Permissions
message: Added JWT authentication with a data-driven permission model, per-user login lockout, and route guarding on the frontend.

whatChanged.text: Added JWT authentication with HTTP-only cookie token storage
Introduced a dynamic requireAccess(predicate) authorization model
items: Permissions are data-driven, not hardcoded per project

whatChanged.text: Added per-user login lockout after repeated failed attempts
Added ProtectedRoute and AuthMiddleware.isAuth() on the frontend
Added the standard auth routes (register, login, refresh, logout)

title: OracleDB Dual-Pool & Mongo-Style Wrapper
message: Connected the template to OracleDB with a resilient dual-pool setup and a MongoDB-style query wrapper that makes Oracle SQL feel familiar.

whatChanged.text: Added an OracleDB adapter with a dual-connection-pool pattern
Added a PoolHealthMonitor (30s checks, 3-strike unhealthy marking)
Added exponential-backoff retry on pool initialization
Added the oracle-mongo-wrapper library (MongoDB-style API over Oracle SQL)
Added Oracle error classification for clear, actionable messages
Added join column disambiguation to prevent ORA-00918 ambiguity errors

title: Rate Limiting, Traceability & Response Timing
message: Added per-IP rate limiting, end-to-end request traceability, and response-time tracking with slow-response detection.

whatChanged.text: Added a Sliding Window Counter rate limiter (in-memory, no Redis)
Added request traceability with a unique X-Request-Id per request
Added response-time tracking with an X-Response-Time header
Added structured incoming and completed request logging
Added graceful shutdown with connection-pool cleanup

title: Aumovio Design System Component Library
message: Shipped the Aumovio Design System — a large, dark-mode-ready React component library covering forms, UI, charts, layout, and typography.

whatChanged.text: Added form components
items: Input, Select, Textarea, Checkbox, Radio, Toggle, FileInput, and more

whatChanged.text: Added UI components
items: Modal, Drawer, Tabs, Table, Card, Badge, Tooltip, Datepicker, and more

whatChanged.text: Added a charts suite (Area, Bar, Donut, Heatmap, Line, Radial, Scatter)
Added layout components (Navbar, Sidebar, Footer, BottomNav)
Added a dark-mode ThemeToggle with persisted preference
Wrapped every view in an ErrorBoundary

title: Domain-Agnostic Cache Subsystem
message: Added a reusable cache subsystem with a registry, deterministic key builder, and cache-aside middleware that ports cleanly to any project.

whatChanged.text: Added CacheStore — a NodeCache wrapper with structured operation logging
Added CacheRegistry — the single place where cache stores are created
Added CacheKeyBuilder — alphabetically sorted keys, auto-hashed when long
Added CacheMiddleware — cache-aside read with fire-and-forget invalidation
items: Only caches 2xx JSON responses; errors are never stored

title: Metrics & Health Observability
message: Added live request metrics and a health endpoint so the running service can be monitored at a glance.

whatChanged.text: Added a metrics middleware tracking Requests, Errors, and Duration (RED)
Added an in-memory MetricsStore with a metrics API route
Added a GET /api/v1/health endpoint that always returns 200 OK
Added frontend charts to visualize live service metrics

title: Audit Logging Module
message: Added an audit logging module that records system activity for traceability and later investigation.

whatChanged.text: Added an audit-log middleware that records mutating requests
Added audit-log route, controller, and service layers
Captured the request actor, action, and timestamp for every audited event

title: RFC 5424 Logging & Session UX
message: Upgraded the logger to the industry-standard RFC 5424 8-level hierarchy and added proactive session-expiry handling on the frontend.

whatChanged.text: Upgraded the logger from 4 levels to the RFC 5424 8-level hierarchy
items: logger.warn(...) renamed to logger.warning(...) — warn kept as a deprecated alias

whatChanged.text: Added a proactive Session Warning modal before the login session expires
Added a Profile modal and toast notifications
Logs are organized as logs/YYYY/MM/DD/level.log and never truncated

title: Theme Personalization
message: Added a Personalize option so each user can pick the app's accent color theme, saved between sessions.

whatChanged.text: Added a ColorPicker-based Personalize option for the accent color theme
Theme preference is persisted between sessions
Replaced hardcoded color values with design-system palette variables for consistency

title: Changelog / Version History Module
message: Added the Changelog module — an encrypted entry store on the backend and a timeline-style Version History page on the frontend.

whatChanged.text: Added an AES-256-GCM encrypted changelog store (data/changelog.enc)
Added changelog routes with SUPER_ADMIN-only create, update, and delete
Added a Version History timeline page with collapsible 'What Changed' sections
Added SemVer-aware version auto-suggestion based on the selected change type

title: Changelog Entry Format Upgrade
message: Restructured changelog entries from a single summary field into a short headline message plus a structured 'What Changed' list, with automatic migration of older entries.

whatChanged.text: Replaced the single summary field with a message + structured whatChanged array
items: whatChanged supports nested sub-bullets (indent 2 spaces in the form)

whatChanged.text: Added automatic, idempotent migration of legacy summary-only entries on read
Updated the entry form to a one-item-per-line 'What Changed' textarea

title: Theme Personalization & Version History Upgrade
message: Personalize the app with a full color picker, more accent palettes that adapt to dark mode, and a layout switcher. The Version History page now shows grouped "what changed" detail per release.

whatChanged.text: Appearance
items: Added a full color picker (drag, hue slider, hex entry, keyboard control)
Added many more accent palettes that adjust their surface and text colors in dark mode

whatChanged.text: Added a layout switcher and a redesigned two-column Personalize panel with live preview
Transparency toggle now applies instantly with no flash on load
Refreshed components for consistent dark-mode colors
Version History
items: Release entries now carry a short headline plus a grouped "what changed" list with sub-items
Reset/seed the history from a standalone script instead of bundled data

title: Accurate Availability and Error Rate Metrics
message: Availability no longer counts client errors (4xx) as failures, so the dashboard reflects true service health. Client and server errors now report as separate, clearly labelled rates.

whatChanged.text: Availability is now computed from server errors only — a 4xx (bad input, failed auth, blocked scanner) counts as a correct rejection, not an outage
Client Error Rate (4xx) and Server Error Rate (5xx) shown as independent metrics, each as a share of total traffic
Added an Availability headline to the system health banner
Fixed audit statistics so category counts always reconcile with the total (no more Success + Redirect exceeding Total)
Retuned the high-error-rate alert to fire on server errors only (1% warning, 5% critical), removing false alarms from client-error noise
Request-completion logs now record the HTTP status code and duration, making error entries diagnosable
Added unit tests covering status-code boundaries and metric reconciliation

title: Accurate Heap Metrics And Leak Detection
message: The system monitor now reports memory against Node's real heap ceiling instead of a misleading internal figure, so a healthy server no longer shows a near-full heap. New garbage-collection and leak-trend indicators make memory problems visible before they cause an outage.

whatChanged.text: Heap usage now measured against the real V8 limit, not the committed heap
items: Dashboard bar reads true utilization (e.g. ~2%) instead of a false ~94%
Committed heap kept as a secondary garbage-collection pressure hint

whatChanged.text: Added a Memory Trend (Leak Detector) card with Stable / Gathering Data / Leak Suspected status
Added garbage-collection health: overhead %, major/minor counts, recent pause times
Heap alerts now graduate (warning at 75%, critical at 90% of the limit)
New alerts for excessive GC overhead and sustained post-GC memory growth
Fixed a broken critical-heap log call that would have thrown when triggered
Added full unit, integration, and chaos test coverage plus test:metrics and test:chaos scripts
